factors.name: US-Iran Peace Deal
factors.summary: US-Iran interim peace deal advancing toward formal signing eases geopolitical risk premium embedded in gold. Oil has shed >7% across two sessions, signaling reduced inflation/haven bid for precious metals.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: No explicit TIPS yield data in news window; however, falling oil prices and reduced inflation expectations from Iran deal typically support lower real yields, which is bullish for gold. Awaiting FOMC clarity on rate trajectory.

factors.name: Fed Policy (FOMC Decision Today)
factors.summary: Kevin Warsh's first FOMC decision imminent; market expects hold, but new Chair's initial communication will be critical for gold. Options traders split on near-term rate path; no hawkish surprise apparent in positioning, but high uncertainty.

factors.name: US Dollar (DXY)
factors.summary: USD weakening on falling oil prices and lack of rate hike expectations. Weaker dollar is structurally bullish for gold, but magnitude unclear; DXY near 99.50.

factors.name: ECB/BoJ Policy
factors.summary: ECB signaled one more hike in September and higher-for-longer rates through 2027, supporting euro and real yields globally. BoJ hiked; yen weak despite, with USD/JPY near 160.47 (intervention threshold), signaling carry-trade dynamics still bearish for gold.

factors.name: Oil Prices (Brent/WTI)
factors.summary: Oil collapsed >7% on Iran deal news, now at three-month lows. Lower energy prices reduce inflation expectations and near-term safe-haven demand for gold, weakening bullish inflation narrative.

factors.name: Equity Risk Sentiment
factors.summary: NASDAQ fell -1.15% on Tuesday; S&P down -0.57%. Tech weakness and risk-off sentiment typically compete with gold safe-haven flows. SpaceX/AI euphoria dominating, not classic risk-off environment.

factors.name: Geopolitical De-Escalation
factors.summary: US-Iran peace deal framework removes a major source of geopolitical risk premium that supported gold. Physical demand from risk hedging likely to ease in near term.

factors.name: Japan Economic Data
factors.summary: Japan exports +17.0% y/y in May (beat +16.2% expected), business sentiment rising on semiconductor demand. Stronger Japan data neutral to slightly positive for yen stability and regional growth, no major gold implication.

factors.name: Price Action Momentum
factors.summary: Gold up only +0.04% in 4h and +0.26% in 24h—minimal momentum despite Iran de-escalation news. Move has not confirmed bullish follow-through; consolidating at resistance near $4,335.

screening.narrative: Gold is consolidating near $4,355 after a modest bounce from multi-week lows, caught between competing narratives: the US-Iran peace deal and falling oil prices are removing geopolitical risk and inflation fears (bearish for gold), while a weakening US Dollar and Fed rate-hold expectations (via Warsh's first decision today) should support it. The lack of aggressive price action (+0.26% in 24h) suggests the market is in a holding pattern ahead of the FOMC announcement and waiting for real-yields guidance. Until the Fed clarifies its rate path and inflation trajectory, gold remains range-bound, with downside risks from prolonged geopolitical de-escalation and upside capped by softer risk sentiment in equities.
catalysts_ahead: FOMC Policy Decision & Warsh Statement (today, ~18:00 UTC)
Fed Chair Warsh's Q&A commentary on rates, inflation, QT pace
US inflation expectations revision post-Iran deal
Oil prices continued weakness/EIA inventory data
USD/JPY intervention risk if pair stays >161 (affects carry unwind, could spike volatility)
Next CPI/PCE print timing and expectations for June
screening.risk_warning: If FOMC signals hawkish surprise (rate hikes rather than holds, or faster QT), or if Iran deal collapses triggering geopolitical re-escalation, this bias flips sharply bullish for gold. Conversely, if Fed dovish guidance becomes entrenched AND equity risk sentiment suddenly turns deeply negative, gold could sell off as real yields fall while equities crater (risk-off without safe-haven bid). BoJ intervention or USD/JPY reversal above 162 could spike volatility unpredictably.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is underway but shallow; recommend WAIT for FOMC clarity before committing. On retest of $4,330–$4,340 support post-decision, reassess bias based on real-yield reaction. Do not chase current consolidation; late entry risk is elevated given imminent catalyst.

factors.name: Real yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: No fresh TIPS data in news window. Market awaits Fed decision today (Warsh) which will anchor real yield expectations; any surprise dovish tilt would be bullish gold.

factors.name: Fed policy decision (TODAY)
factors.summary: FOMC decision imminent (later 17 Jun). No change to rates expected per investinglive summary; market will focus on dot plot and forward guidance under new Chair Kevin Warsh. High uncertainty until release.

factors.name: USD strength (DXY)
factors.summary: DXY weakened to near 99.50 on US-Iran peace deal optimism. Weaker dollar is bullish gold, but move appears partially priced in already (mentioned as repositioning ahead of FOMC).

factors.name: Inflation data (UK CPI)
factors.summary: UK May CPI held at +2.8% y/y (vs +3.0% expected), missing upside. Core CPI +2.6% y/y vs +2.7% expected. Cool inflation is mildly bullish gold but UK-specific, not US inflation trend.

factors.name: Geopolitical risk (US-Iran deal)
factors.summary: US-Iran framework agreement including $300bn fund and ceasefire relief; Strait of Hormuz reopening reducing oil-driven inflation fears. Lower energy risk = lower Fed hike risk = supportive for gold longer-term, but near-term uncertainty on implementation.

factors.name: Oil prices (Brent/WTI)
factors.summary: WTI near $75.80 (down ~5% previous day) on Hormuz reopening hopes and Iran supply returning. Lower oil reduces inflation pressure, which is ultimately bearish gold (less Fed urgency to hike = lower real yields, but also lower risk premium).

factors.name: Central bank policy (BoJ, ECB)
factors.summary: BoJ raised rates to 3-decade highs; ECB officials signaling more hikes ahead despite peace deal. BoJ tightening supports JPY (pressures USD/JPY below 160.50), mildly supportive for gold. ECB hawkishness is neutral to slightly bearish.

factors.name: Risk sentiment
factors.summary: Mixed signals: peace deal optimism supporting risk-on (EUR/USD +0.1610, AUD/USD above 0.7050), but traders cautious ahead of FOMC. Gold trading 'with mild negative bias' per FXStreet, suggesting some risk-on unwind of safe-haven premium.

factors.name: Gold ETF/physical flows
factors.summary: No specific GLD or physical demand data in window. Rabobank notes rising central bank gold demand and de-dollarization as structural bid, but no new positioning flow data.

factors.name: Price action (XAU/USD)
factors.summary: Gold edges lower during Asian session, holding above $4,300 but with 'mild negative bias' per FXStreet. Bulls lightening bets ahead of FOMC, suggesting uncertainty and lack of conviction into event.

screening.narrative: Gold is in a holding pattern ahead of the Fed decision today (Warsh FOMC). The US-Iran peace deal framework is reducing geopolitical premium and oil prices, which cuts into inflation expectations and limits gold's safe-haven appeal in the near term. Simultaneously, weaker USD on peace optimism provides modest support. However, traders are lightening long positions ahead of the event, and price action shows 'mild negative bias.' The outcome depends critically on whether the Fed signals continued or accelerated rate cuts — dovish surprise would flip bias bullish; hawkish surprise would extend weakness.
catalysts_ahead: Fed FOMC decision + dot plot + Warsh forward guidance (TODAY, 17 Jun ~18:00 UTC)
US May Retail Sales (concurrent with FOMC, 17 Jun)
Potential US-Iran deal text release (pending)
ECB next meeting (TBD, but officials already hawkish)
NFP (likely next Friday 20 Jun)
screening.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if FOMC signals dovish pivot (rate cuts sooner/more aggressive) — would trigger immediate bullish reversal. Conversely, if Warsh surprises hawkish or holds hawkish forward guidance, gold could break below $4,285. Hormuz reopening execution delay or renegotiation could flip geopolitical risk back on, supporting gold. Move in gold already underway (price softening), so entry into further weakness requires conviction on Fed outcome.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is already in progress (mild downside). Avoid chasing; wait for FOMC decision at ~18:00 UTC, then enter on retest of $4,300 support (if result is hawkish) or on pullback toward $4,310–$4,315 if dovish surprise occurs. Do NOT enter now given imminent catalyst and repositioning chop.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: No explicit TIPS data in news window, but US Treasury yields expected to remain sticky given hawkish Fed guidance and resilient growth. Oil price collapse (Brent below $79) reduces near-term inflation risk, which should support lower real yields. Conflicting signals present.

factors.name: Federal Reserve Policy (FOMC Today)
factors.summary: FOMC decision expected to keep rates unchanged at 3.50–3.75% with unanimous removal of easing bias per FXStreet preview. New Chair Kevin Warsh guidance is critical; markets already pricing no cuts for remainder of 2026 per dot plot expectations. Warsh's inaugural presser is a wildcard—could shift dollar/yield sentiment materially.

factors.name: Oil Prices & Inflation Expectations
factors.summary: Brent crude crashed below $79 (15% down over 4 sessions) on US-Iran ceasefire deal framework; WTI near $75.50. This sharp energy disinflation should ease real yield pressures and reduce Fed tightening conviction, which is constructive for gold. MUFG notes 30% crude drop significantly reduced near-term inflation risk perception.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: Dollar consolidating after giving back >50% of post-May payrolls gains per BBH. Oil recovery/geopolitical risk dissipation typically benefits higher-yielding assets and weakens USD safe-haven bid. ING/MUFG note cyclical bullishness in FX reasserting, but oil collapse may cap DXY near-term strength.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (US-Iran Deal)
factors.summary: Trump surprised markets by cancelling planned Iran strikes and announcing ceasefire framework; formal MoU signing Friday in Switzerland. Iran to receive $300B development fund and immediate oil export rights. Risk-off premium collapsing, reducing safe-haven demand for gold but deflating inflation expectations via energy.

factors.name: Gold ETF Flows & Institutional Positioning
factors.summary: No explicit GLD/IAU flow data in news window. Investor sentiment appears cautious ahead of FOMC decision; UOB notes gold advanced on easing Fed hike expectations, but moves remain tentative.

factors.name: Central Bank Policy (ECB, BoE, BoJ)
factors.summary: BoJ hiked 25bp to 1.0% with tapering confirmation for 2027 (limited JPY support per OCBC); RBA held at 4.35% with hawkish pause signal; ECB rate hike fully priced, limiting EUR upside per ING. Mixed signals, no clear directional impetus.

factors.name: Inflation Data (Recent CPI Prints)
factors.summary: Euro area headline CPI confirmed at 3.2% in May (up from 3.0% April), highest since Sept 2023, with energy +10.8% y/y. This hot print in Eurozone contradicts emerging disinflationary narrative elsewhere and may keep ECB hawkish, supporting EUR/DXY and pressuring gold.

factors.name: Price Action & Trend Confirmation
factors.summary: Gold up only +0.03% over 4h and -0.01% over 24h—minimal directional movement despite major catalyst (Iran deal, Fed meeting hours away). No trending setup; market in wait-and-see mode ahead of FOMC.

factors.name: VIX & Risk Sentiment
factors.summary: Equities mixed: Nasdaq under pressure (awaiting FOMC), S&P 500 futures +0.26%, Dow futures flat. Risk-off premium has deflated post-Iran deal; gold typically underperforms in low-volatility consolidations.

screening.narrative: Gold is caught in a tug-of-war between two forces: disinflationary impulses from the US-Iran ceasefire (oil down 15% in 4 sessions, Brent below $79) which should lower real yields and support bullion, versus persistent Fed hawkishness and dollar resilience as new Chair Warsh prepares his first post-FOMC press conference today. Eurozone CPI surprising hot (+3.2% in May vs +3.0% April) adds a counterintuitive tightening bias in Europe, which could support the dollar near-term. The dominant story is one of shock geopolitical risk relief colliding with unchanged restrictive monetary policy—a recipe for sideways consolidation until FOMC guidance clarifies the Fed's true inflation/growth view.
catalysts_ahead: FOMC decision + Warsh presser (today, late US morning) — critical for real yields and dot plot guidance
Summary of Economic Projections (SEP) dot plot — markets expect no cuts for 2026, possibly more hawkish dots
US-Iran deal formal signing (Friday, Switzerland) — could extend oil/risk rally if framework holds
PCE/Core PCE inflation expectations if released in next 72h — major confirmation of disinflationary trend
China-related data or trade war headlines — structural headwind to growth expectations
screening.risk_warning: FOMC could deliver surprise hawkish guidance (e.g. Warsh signals aggressive stance on inflation, lifting real yields and USD), which would invalidate the disinflationary gold thesis and trigger a sharp pullback below $4,300. Conversely, if Iran deal falls apart or geopolitical tensions re-escalate, oil could spike, reigniting inflation concerns and forcing gold higher despite hawkish Fed tone—watch for unexpected Trump/Iran rhetoric shifts.

factors.name: Fed Policy (Warsh debut, no rate change expected)
factors.summary: FOMC expected to hold rates at 3.50%-3.75% in unanimous decision and remove easing bias per FXStreet. Dot plot and Warsh commentary are the wild cards—Fidelity warns he may stoke bond volatility with inflation views, but this is a 'show, don't tell' event happening TODAY, limiting certainty.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: No fresh TIPS yield data in news window. US 10y nominal yields mixed ahead of FOMC; without explicit TIPS breakout, real yield direction remains ambiguous. This is the single biggest gold driver but currently opaque.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: USD mixed on Fed Day per Investinglive. EUR retreating below 1.1600, NZD weakness, USD/CAD grinding higher, but no decisive directional signal. BoJ hike to 1.0% has NOT supported JPY carry-off (yen still weak), limiting USD/JPY upside as gold headwind.

factors.name: Oil Prices & Geopolitical Risk
factors.summary: US-Iran framework deal announced; Strait of Hormuz reopening, oil inventory draws fading, WTI retreated from session highs. Iraq preparing to boost exports. Trump's hawkish 'go right back to dropping bombs' comment is theater—market is pricing in de-escalation. Lower oil → lower inflation expectations → potentially lower real yields (bullish gold) but sentiment is risk-on (bearish gold).

factors.name: Inflation Data (Recent Prints)
factors.summary: US retail sales beat: +0.9% MoM vs +0.5% expected (May); control group +0.7% vs +0.4%. Euro inflation accelerated in May (services stubborn). No fresh CPI/PCE in window, but retail beat suggests sticky demand and inflation durability—this could support real yields and weigh on gold.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment & Equities
factors.summary: S&P 500 worst session in 8 months days after record highs; tech mixed (Broadcom up, Microsoft down, SpaceX down 5% post-IPO). Fed Day choppy. Risk-on partially resumed but fragile. When equities rally (as they may on dovish surprises), gold often underperforms—currently equities slight bid, pressuring gold.

factors.name: Central Bank Buying & ETF Flows
factors.summary: No fresh CB buying or GLD/IAU flow data in 24h news window. Structural bid from PBoC/RBI intact but invisible in near-term price action.

factors.name: Inflation Expectations
factors.summary: ECB's Sleijpen: '2022 inflation repeat less likely but can't be excluded.' No Michigan survey or breakeven data in window. Expectations appear stable, neither bullish nor bearish for gold in isolation.

factors.name: Economic Data Quality
factors.summary: Pending home sales +3.8% MoM (vs +0.8% expected)—surprise upside. Retail beat. But no major downside data flipped. Economy data mixed, supporting 'muddle-through' view. Real yields likely to drift, not spike.

factors.name: Price Action Momentum
factors.summary: 4h change +0.29%, 24h change +0.46%—minimal momentum. Three consecutive NEUTRAL 5/10 reads before this (2026-06-17 00:14, 06:09, 11:46) confirm lack of directional confirmation. No trend yet.

screening.narrative: Gold is in a holding pattern ahead of Kevin Warsh's first FOMC decision (expected to hold rates, remove easing bias, and possibly omit the dot plot). The US-Iran framework deal is pricing in de-escalation risk—oil lower, inflation expectations stable-to-fading, and real yields likely to stay range-bound. Retail sales beat (+0.9%) suggests consumer durability and sticky inflation, which could justify higher-for-longer rates, but the Fed is unlikely to signal hikes today. With no clear Fed surprise priced in and risk sentiment fragile (S&P 500 volatility, SpaceX profit-taking), gold is caught between a dovish playbook (lower real yields → bullish) and a risk-on unwind (equities strength → bearish). The bias is NEUTRAL with low conviction until post-FOMC clarity.
catalysts_ahead: FOMC decision & statement (TODAY, ~18:00 UTC, 2:00 PM ET) — Warsh's tone on inflation, removal of easing bias, dot plot omission status
Post-FOMC Fed Chair press conference — real-time inflation/rates expectations reset
Oil inventory data continuation — if draws continue, inflation narrative shifts
US 10y TIPS yield moves — single biggest gold driver; watch 10y nominal and breakeven separately
Equity VIX/risk appetite reversal — if S&P recovers, gold could face headwind; if sharp dip, flight-to-safety
screening.risk_warning: FOMC surprise hawkishness (e.g., Warsh signals 'higher-for-longer' or market implies rate hike risk) would spike real yields and crush gold; conversely, dovish surprise or delayed hike signaling would trigger bullish gold breakout. Currently no 'surprise' is baked in, making any FOMC reaction outsized. Additionally, if geopolitical risk resurfaces (Iran deal collapses, Trump escalates), oil/inflation expectations flip and gold could surge despite USD strength.
screening.entry_guidance: Wait for post-FOMC dust to settle (30-60 min after press conference) and retest of support ($4,350–$4,360) or resistance ($4,380–$4,400) before committing. Move has NOT started; no entry warranted in front of event. If FOMC dovish, long gold on dips. If hawkish, short on any relief rallies. Currently too early to chase in either direction.

factors.name: Fed Policy — Warsh Debut
factors.summary: FOMC held rates at 3.50–3.75% as expected, but removed easing bias language from statement (per Bloomberg/FRB). New Chair Warsh withheld dot plot projections. Dot plot update shows end-2026 target at 3.8% vs 3.4% in March — a 40bp hawkish repricing. This removes prior dovish guidance and signals higher-for-longer, headwind for gold.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: No explicit TIPS yield data in news, but hawkish Fed repricing (higher rate expectations) typically lifts real yields, which is bearish for gold. However, move is modest and uncertain without current snapshot.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: No DXY quote in news, but USD strength typically follows hawkish Fed surprise. Warsh's tighter stance should support USD near-term. GBP weakness vs USD noted (1.3400), AUD resilient on geopolitical improvement, suggesting mixed DXY picture — not decisively bullish for gold.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk — Iran Deal
factors.summary: US-Iran peace deal signed/pending signature Friday (per Trump). Strait of Hormuz threat level downgraded by maritime security group. Reduced Middle East tail risk typically bullish for risk assets and commodities; oil (WTI) at $75.70 (-0.22%) shows mild relief. However, Trump's caveat ('might not sign') adds uncertainty.

factors.name: Oil Prices (WTI/Brent)
factors.summary: WTI $75.70, down 0.22% on day despite Iran deal progress. Oil not rallying post-deal, suggesting limited immediate inflation shock relief or concerns about deal durability. Neutral for gold; no strong inflation-via-oil vector.

factors.name: Inflation Expectations & Breakevens
factors.summary: No fresh CPI/PCE/inflation print in news window. Last inflation data is implicit in Fed concern about 'elevated' inflation in statement. No Michigan survey, no 5y5y breakeven update. Lacking real-time inflation data to drive gold positioning.

factors.name: Price Action / Momentum
factors.summary: Gold +0.77% (FXStreet pre-FOMC), now at $4,352.80 with -0.70% in last 4h and +0.15% in 24h. Move has already occurred pre-FOMC (rally into event). Post-FOMC (hawkish surprise with no dots), price action shows reversion: 4h decline of -0.70% suggests sellers stepping in after initial relief trade unwound.

factors.name: Safe-Haven Flows / Risk Sentiment
factors.summary: Iran deal reducing geopolitical tail risk favors risk-on (equities, commodities). Retail flows into SpaceX and equities noted. No VIX print, but sentiment appears mixed—not a full risk-off, risk-on mode is tentative pending deal finalization and broader macro clarity.

factors.name: Central Bank Guidance — Warsh Tone
factors.summary: Warsh said to be 'destined to disappoint Trump' (Bloomberg) — implying hawkish bias vs Trump's preference for cuts. Unanimity on FOMC (12-0 vote) masks internal split on rate path (dot plot shows 2026 end target 3.8%, implying potential hikes, vs March's dovish 3.4%). Hawkish messaging suppresses gold.

factors.name: Upcoming Data Risk
factors.summary: Philadelphia Fed Manufacturing Index and jobless claims due Thursday. No major inflation print or NFP until next week. Limited catalyst density in next 24-48h; markets settling into new Warsh regime uncertainty.

screening.narrative: Gold rallied +0.77% into Warsh's FOMC debut on hopes of softer guidance, but the Fed surprised by removing dovish language and repricing 2026 rate expectations from 3.4% to 3.8% — a hawkish shift. The new Fed Chair withheld dot plots, signaling tighter control and potentially higher rates ahead. Post-announcement, gold has reversed -0.70% in 4h as sellers test the intraday highs, indicating the move into the event is now being partially unwound. Geopolitical tailwind from Iran deal (Strait of Hormuz reopening, reduced conflict risk) provides some offset, but real-yields headwind from Warsh's hawkish repricing dominates. Phase 2: move underway, initial relief trade reversing.
catalysts_ahead: Philadelphia Fed Manufacturing Index, jobless claims (Thursday)
Iran deal signing Friday (Trump said Friday or Saturday) — confirmation/any new delays could shift risk sentiment
CPI/PCE or other inflation data (likely next week, specific dates unclear from this window)
Any Warsh or other FOMC member hawkish/dovish commentary in next 24-48h
screening.risk_warning: If Fed signals actual rate cuts or easing bias in coming weeks, or if Iran deal fails and geopolitical tension spikes sharply, gold breaks above $4,360 and rallies. Conversely, if real yields rise further or if USD accelerates higher (DXY >105), gold breaks below $4,320 and tests $4,280. Major inflation surprise next week could flip the entire narrative.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is extended into and off FOMC news; avoid chasing lower. Wait for consolidation below $4,340 for potential short entry on retest of resistance, or hold for a clear break below $4,320 support if hawkish momentum sustains. Risk/reward currently unfavorable; next event (Philly Fed, CPI) may offer better setup.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: 10Y nominal Treasury yield rose +2.7 bps to 4.4552% post-FOMC. More significantly, the market is now pricing a rate hike by October 2026 per 'Traders Fully Price in a Rate Hike by October' (Bloomberg, 18:14). Rising real yields are the single largest headwind for gold.

factors.name: Fed Policy Shift (Warsh FOMC)
factors.summary: Kevin Warsh's first FOMC meeting delivered a hawkish surprise: dot plot raised end-2026 rate projection to 3.8% vs 3.4% in March; statement removed dovish cutting bias; Warsh emphasized 'deliver price stability' and 'inflation running well ahead of 2%' with no cutting guidance. This is a stark 180° from prior dovish assumptions.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: USD/JPY rallied to 160.79 (highest since 2024 per FXStreet); DXI 'storms higher' and 'storms back' per multiple sources (18:15, 18:17); EUR/USD 'routed' and 'folds' (18:18). Stronger USD is inversely correlated with gold.

factors.name: 2Y Treasury Yield
factors.summary: 2Y yield spiked +9.1 bps to 4.1379% at time of press conference per investinglive (18:36). Front-end yields rising sharply signal market pricing of near-term hikes, which compresses real yields further and pressures gold.

factors.name: Inflation Expectations & PCE Projections
factors.summary: Fed lifted PCE inflation projections modestly; Warsh stated 'inflation has been running well ahead of 2%' but no fresh CPI/PCE print in the news window. FOMC confirmed 'we have the capability to deliver on 2%', suggesting hawkish resolve rather than inflation surprise.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Iran Deal & Oil)
factors.summary: US-Iran ceasefire agreement confirmed with $300bn reconstruction deal; Strait of Hormuz threat downgraded; oil market implications stabilizing rather than inflationary. This removes a recent bullish catalyst for gold (inflation hedge).

factors.name: Equity Market Reaction
factors.summary: S&P 500 down -0.54%, Dow down -0.17%, Nasdaq down -0.56% during press conference window. Risk-off tone weakens the 'safe-haven' bid for gold vs equities. However, not a sharp selloff—modest pressure suggests some transition out of equities into USD rather than a flight-to-safety into gold.

factors.name: Price Action Momentum
factors.summary: Gold has already declined -2.81% in 4h and -1.58% in 24h. The move is in-progress (Phase 2), indicating the hawkish FOMC catalyst has already triggered selling. XAU/USD trading 'volatilely within $4,330–$4,280 range' per FXStreet (18:15).

factors.name: BoJ Currency Intervention Risk
factors.summary: USD/JPY at 160.79 risks BoJ intervention to weaken yen; BoJ officials have signaled caution above 160.00. Any intervention would reverse USD strength temporarily, supporting gold. However, no intervention occurred yet; Warsh's hawkish stance may override BoJ pushback near-term.

factors.name: Gold ETF Flows & CB Buying
factors.summary: No fresh ETF flow data or central bank gold purchase news in the 24h window. Structural CB bid is ongoing but insufficient to offset the hawkish real-yield headwind.

screening.narrative: Kevin Warsh's debut as Fed Chair delivered a hawkish shock: the dot plot now projects rates at 3.8% by year-end 2026 (up from 3.4% in March), and markets are pricing a rate hike by October. Real yields are rising sharply as 10Y nominal yields drift higher and inflation expectations remain sticky. The USD has rallied strongly (USD/JPY to 160.79, EUR/USD lower), which inversely pressures gold. The 2.81% 4h decline and 1.58% 24h fall indicate the move is already underway (Phase 2), with gold caught in a coordinated selloff driven by real-yield normalization and hawkish Fed surprise—the exact opposite of the dovish soft-landing narrative many had positioned for.
catalysts_ahead: New Zealand Q1 GDP (Thursday 18 June, early Asia session) — potential risk-off if disappoints, minor for gold
Fed speakers (likely this week) — risk of further hawkish rhetoric or pushback from Warsh
US inflation data (CPI/PCE) — next major release typically late June; if hot, extends bearish bias for gold
Treasury/BoJ dynamics — watch for BoJ intervention or comments; could flip USD weakness and support gold
Oil price stability — Iran deal reduces inflation premium; lack of energy shock removes bullish case
screening.risk_warning: Invalidation: (1) Sharp BoJ intervention if USD/JPY breaks above 161 could reverse USD strength and spark a gold bounce. (2) A significant equity market selloff (VIX spike, S&P breakdown) could trigger flight-to-safety demand that overwhelms real-yield headwinds. (3) An unexpected dovish or inflation-pivot statement from a Fed speaker (Barkin, Kasriel, etc.) in the next 48h could unwind the hawkish repricing. (4) Geopolitical escalation (Iran–Israel, Ukraine) could restore safe-haven bid. (5) If the 4,250 support breaks decisively with high volume, target $4,200–$4,150.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in Phase 2 (extended 2.8% lower in 4h). Avoid chasing shorts here; wait for a pullback/retest of $4,300–$4,320 resistance or a stabilization at $4,250–$4,270 support before re-establishing short exposure. If you are long, consider taking profits into any intraday bounce.

factors.name: Federal Reserve Policy
factors.summary: Warsh's first FOMC held rates at 3.50%-3.75% but delivered hawkish surprise via dot plot showing end-2026 rate at 3.8% vs 3.4% in March, signaling potential rate HIKE this year. Statement removed all dovish cutting bias. Warsh: 'We recognize inflation has been running well ahead of 2%' and 'I see no reason to revisit 2% inflation goal until we have delivered.'

factors.name: Real Yields
factors.summary: 2Y Treasury yield rocketed +9.1bps to 4.1379% and 5Y yield +6.4bps to 4.2146% immediately post-FOMC, indicating rising real yields as market reprices for potential hike vs cuts. This is the single most negative factor for gold.

factors.name: US Dollar Index
factors.summary: Bloomberg reports 'Dollar surged the most in three months' and 'Dollar rallies as Fed officials open door to 2026 rate hike.' USD strength directly pressures gold prices. Markets fully pricing in rate hike by October per Bloomberg.

factors.name: USD/JPY
factors.summary: USDJPY extended above 2026 high at 160.717, reaching 160.79, hitting weakest level vs dollar since July 2024. This broad dollar strength reflects hawkish Fed repricing and competes with gold as safe-haven.

factors.name: Fed Forward Guidance Shift
factors.summary: Warsh drastically altered Fed statement, removing forward guidance entirely but dot plot showed officials' hawkish hand. Task forces announced signal institutional shift. Gundlach noted 'Warsh is not going to be the easy money chairman many hoped for.'

factors.name: Market Positioning
factors.summary: US stocks fell (S&P -0.54%, Nasdaq -0.56%) on hawkish Fed, indicating risk-off but driven by dollar strength rather than safe-haven gold demand. Gold declining -2.81% in 4h suggests institutional liquidation underway.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Iran)
factors.summary: US-Iran MOU expected to be signed Friday per multiple reports, with Strait of Hormuz threat downgraded per US-led maritime security group. This removes geopolitical premium from gold, though deal signing still uncertain per Trump comments. Hormuz tolling regime adds structural oil premium but immediate war risk fading.

factors.name: Inflation Expectations
factors.summary: Fed's hawkish pivot signals elevated inflation concerns ('well ahead of 2%'). Higher inflation typically bullish for gold long-term, but near-term the Fed's aggressive response (potential hikes) dominates via real yields and dollar strength.

factors.name: Oil Prices
factors.summary: Iran deal confirmation and $300bn reconstruction deal confirmed per news, with permanent Hormuz changes adding structural premium to crude. Mixed signal: lower immediate war risk but persistent tolling costs create new inflationary input.

factors.name: Treasury Curve
factors.summary: Front-end yields surging more than long-end (2Y +9.1bps vs 10Y +2.7bps) indicates bear flattening on hawkish repricing. BlackRock's Rosenberg warns about 'overplaying the flattening yield curve' but move is real-time bearish for gold.

confirmation.narrative: Warsh's first FOMC delivered a major hawkish shock: dot plot now shows 3.8% year-end rate (implying a hike), statement stripped of all dovish bias, and Chair explicitly said no reason to revisit 2% target until delivered. Markets repriced aggressively—2Y yields jumped 9bps, dollar surged most in 3 months, and gold fell -2.81% in 4 hours. Real yields rising and dollar strength create powerful bearish confluence for gold. Iran deal reducing geopolitical premium adds to downside pressure.
catalysts_ahead: US-Iran MOU signing expected Friday June 19
New Zealand Q1 GDP Thursday
Fed task force announcements 'in next couple weeks'
Any Fed official speeches following Warsh's hawkish reset
confirmation.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if: (1) dollar reverses sharply lower on profit-taking, (2) Iran deal collapses and geopolitical premium returns, (3) upcoming economic data (jobs, inflation) forces Fed to walk back hawkish stance, or (4) gold reclaims $4,330 resistance decisively. Move already 2.81% in 4h—entry should wait for bounce/retest.
confirmation.entry_guidance: Move underway and extended (-2.81% in 4h). Wait for retest of $4,300-$4,320 resistance zone before considering shorts, or look for continuation below $4,250 support with tighter stops.

reason: DISAGREEMENT: Haiku=SELL vs Sonnet=BEARISH — silent log
